main_workflow.name: User Authentication and Authorization Flow
main_workflow.description: The core security workflow that handles user authentication (login), JWT token generation/validation, and role-based access control (RBAC) enforcement across the entire microservices platform. This workflow implements a dual-token mechanism (access token + refresh token) with Redis-backed session management and blacklist capabilities. It serves as the foundation for all protected operations in the system, ensuring secure identity verification and permission enforcement for patients, doctors, and family members accessing the chronic disease care platform.
main_workflow.flowchart_mermaid:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Client["Client Application"]
        A["User submits credentials\n(username/phone/ID card + password)"]
    end
    
    subgraph Gateway["API Gateway Domain"]
        B["TraceAndJwtAuthFilter\nReceives login request"]
        C["Route to auth-consumer\n(login endpoints whitelisted)"]
        M["Validate JWT Token\nvia TokenValidationAPI"]
        N["Inject User Context Headers\nX-User-Id, X-Username\nX-User-Roles, X-User-Authorities"]
    end
    
    subgraph AuthDomain["Authentication & Authorization Domain"]
        D["SSOController\nReceives login request"]
        E["SSOServiceImpl\nProcess authentication"]
        K["JwtUtils.generateTokenPair()\nCreate Access Token (30min)\nCreate Refresh Token (7 days)"]
        L["Store Refresh Token\nin Redis"]
        P["TokenValidationServiceImpl\nValidate token signature\nCheck expiration & blacklist"]
    end
    
    subgraph AccountDomain["Account Management Domain"]
        F["AccountServiceImpl.login()\nQuery by username/phone hash/ID hash"]
        G["Verify BCrypt password"]
        H["Query AccountRole table\nGet user role codes"]
        I["PermissionServiceImpl\ngetUserPermission()"]
        J["PermissionExpandUtil\nExpand roles to permissions\n(e.g., DOCTOR:READ, DOCTOR:WRITE)"]
    end
    
    subgraph TargetService["Target Microservice"]
        O["UserInfoExtractInterceptor\nExtract headers to ThreadLocal"]
        Q["PermissionCheckAspect\n@RequireRole / @RequirePermission"]
        R["Business Logic Execution"]
        S["UserInfoManager.clearUserContext()\nCleanup ThreadLocal"]
    end
    
    A --> B
    B --> C
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E --> F
    F --> G
    G -->|"Valid"| H
    G -->|"Invalid"| T["Return Authentication Failed"]
    H --> I
    I --> J
    J --> K
    K --> L
    L --> U["Return Token Pair + User Info"]
    
    V["Subsequent API Request\nwith Bearer Token"] --> M
    M --> P
    P -->|"Valid"| N
    P -->|"Invalid/Expired/Blacklisted"| W["Return 401 Unauthorized"]
    N --> O
    O --> Q
    Q -->|"Permission Granted"| R
    Q -->|"Permission Denied"| X["Return 403 Forbidden"]
    R --> S

other_important_workflows.name: Health Report Upload and Management Flow
other_important_workflows.description: A core clinical workflow enabling patients to upload health reports (images, PDFs, or text content) to the platform. The workflow includes multi-layer security validation (role verification, resource ownership checks, file type/size validation), cloud storage integration via Alibaba OSS, automatic association with the patient's attending doctor, and persistence with audit status tracking. This workflow ensures patients can only manage their own reports while doctors can view their assigned patients' reports.
other_important_workflows.flowchart_mermaid:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Client["Patient Client"]
        A["Patient initiates report upload\n(Image/PDF/Text)"]
    end
    
    subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"]
        B["JWT Token Validation"]
        C["Inject User Context Headers"]
    end
    
    subgraph Consumer["Health Report Consumer (BFF)"]
        D["HealthReportController.uploadReport()"]
        E["@RequireRole PATIENT\n@RequirePermission patient:write"]
        F["Validate file type whitelist\n(PNG, JPG, PDF)"]
        G["Validate file size\n(max 10MB)"]
        H["Verify patientId matches\ncurrent user from JWT"]
    end
    
    subgraph Provider["Health Report Provider"]
        I["HealthReportServiceImpl.uploadReport()"]
        J["Validate request parameters"]
        K{"Report Type?"}
    end
    
    subgraph AccountService["Account Management"]
        L["UserIdentityAPI.getPatientInfo()"]
        M["Retrieve attendingDoctorId"]
    end
    
    subgraph ThirdParty["Third-Party Integration"]
        N["OSSAPI.uploadFile()"]
        O["Upload to Alibaba Cloud OSS"]
        P["Return permanent file URL"]
    end
    
    subgraph Persistence["Data Persistence"]
        Q["Build HealthReport entity"]
        R["Set status = PENDING\n(awaiting review)"]
        S["Associate with attending doctor"]
        T["Save to MySQL via MyBatis-Plus"]
    end
    
    A --> B
    B -->|"Valid"| C
    B -->|"Invalid"| Z1["401 Unauthorized"]
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E -->|"Authorized"| F
    E -->|"Denied"| Z2["403 Forbidden"]
    F -->|"Valid type"| G
    F -->|"Invalid type"| Z3["400 Invalid File Type"]
    G -->|"Within limit"| H
    G -->|"Exceeds limit"| Z4["413 File Too Large"]
    H -->|"Ownership verified"| I
    H -->|"Mismatch"| Z5["403 Access Denied"]
    I --> J
    J --> L
    L --> M
    M --> K
    K -->|"IMAGE/PDF"| N
    N --> O
    O --> P
    P --> Q
    K -->|"TEXT"| Q
    Q --> R
    R --> S
    S --> T
    T --> U["Return Success\nwith Report ID"]

other_important_workflows.name: Doctor Schedule Management with AI Recommendations Flow
other_important_workflows.description: A clinical workflow supporting the doctor workbench functionality, including AI-powered schedule recommendations, appointment management, and complete schedule lifecycle management (PENDING → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED/CANCELLED). The workflow implements strict ownership validation, optimistic locking for concurrent updates, and requires diagnostic report submission upon schedule completion. This enables efficient doctor-patient care coordination in chronic disease management.
other_important_workflows.flowchart_mermaid:
flowchart TD
    subgraph DoctorClient["Doctor Workbench Client"]
        A["Doctor requests\nschedule recommendations"]
        B["Doctor queries\nexisting schedules"]
        C["Doctor updates\nschedule status"]
    end
    
    subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"]
        D["JWT Validation"]
        E["Inject Doctor Context"]
    end
    
    subgraph Consumer["Doctor Service Consumer"]
        F["DoctorWorkbenchController"]
        G["@RequireRole DOCTOR"]
        H["@RequirePermission doctor:read/write"]
        I["Override doctorId with\nJWT userId (security)"]
    end
    
    subgraph Provider["Doctor Service Provider"]
        J["DoctorWorkbenchServiceImpl"]
        K["generateScheduleSuggestion()\n(AI Mock Implementation)"]
        L["querySchedule()\nMulti-criteria filter"]
        M["getScheduleDetail()\nWith ownership validation"]
        N["updateScheduleStatus()"]
        O["completeSchedule()"]
        P["cancelSchedule()"]
    end
    
    subgraph StateValidation["State Machine Validation"]
        Q{"Current Status?"}
        R["PENDING"]
        S["IN_PROGRESS"]
        T["Validate state transition"]
    end
    
    subgraph Completion["Schedule Completion"]
        U{"Has diagnostic report?"}
        V["Record diagnosis result"]
        W["Record prescription"]
        X["Set status = COMPLETED"]
    end
    
    subgraph Cancellation["Schedule Cancellation"]
        Y{"Has cancel reason?"}
        ZA["Record cancel reason"]
        ZB["Set status = CANCELLED"]
    end
    
    subgraph Persistence["Data Layer"]
        ZC["Optimistic Lock Check\n(@Version)"]
        ZD["Update DoctorSchedule"]
        ZE["Return ScheduleVO"]
    end
    
    A --> D
    B --> D
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E --> F
    F --> G
    G --> H
    H --> I
    I --> J
    
    J -->|"AI Suggestion"| K
    K --> ZE
    
    J -->|"Query"| L
    L --> ZE
    
    J -->|"Get Detail"| M
    M --> ZE
    
    J -->|"Update Status"| N
    N --> Q
    Q --> R
    R -->|"To IN_PROGRESS"| T
    Q --> S
    S -->|"To COMPLETED"| T
    T --> ZC
    
    J -->|"Complete"| O
    O --> U
    U -->|"Yes"| V
    U -->|"No"| Z1["400 Diagnosis Required"]
    V --> W
    W --> X
    X --> ZC
    
    J -->|"Cancel"| P
    P --> Y
    Y -->|"Yes"| ZA
    Y -->|"No"| Z2["400 Reason Required"]
    ZA --> ZB
    ZB --> ZC
    
    ZC -->|"Version Match"| ZD
    ZC -->|"Conflict"| Z3["409 Concurrent Update"]
    ZD --> ZE

other_important_workflows.name: Token Refresh and Session Extension Flow
other_important_workflows.description: A security workflow enabling seamless session extension without requiring users to re-authenticate. When the short-lived access token (30 minutes) expires, clients can exchange a valid refresh token (7 days) for a new access token. The workflow validates the refresh token against Redis storage, fetches the latest user permissions (which may have changed since initial login), and generates a new token pair. This supports continuous user experience while maintaining security through token rotation.
other_important_workflows.flowchart_mermaid:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Client["Client Application"]
        A["Access Token expired\n(after 30 minutes)"]
        B["Submit Refresh Token\nPOST /auth/refresh"]
    end
    
    subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"]
        C["Route to auth-consumer\n(refresh endpoint whitelisted)"]
    end
    
    subgraph AuthConsumer["Auth Consumer"]
        D["SSOController\nReceives refresh request"]
        E["SSOServiceImpl\nProcess token refresh"]
    end
    
    subgraph AuthProvider["Auth Provider"]
        F["JwtUtils.validateRefreshToken()"]
        G["Decode JWT & verify signature"]
        H{"Token type = 'refresh'?"}
        I["Extract tokenId from claims"]
        J{"Exists in Redis?"}
        K["Extract userId, roles\nfrom token claims"]
    end
    
    subgraph Redis["Redis Cache"]
        L["refresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}"]
        M["Check token existence"]
    end
    
    subgraph AccountDomain["Account Management"]
        N["PermissionServiceImpl\ngetUserPermission()"]
        O["Fetch latest permissions\n(may have changed)"]
        P["PermissionExpandUtil\nExpand to permission strings"]
    end
    
    subgraph TokenGeneration["New Token Generation"]
        Q["JwtUtils.generateAccessToken()\nNew 30-min access token"]
        R{"Rotate refresh token?"}
        S["Generate new refresh token"]
        T["Revoke old refresh token"]
        U["Store new token in Redis"]
    end
    
    subgraph Response["Response"]
        V["Return new token pair"]
        W["Return new access token only"]
    end
    
    A --> B
    B --> C
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E --> F
    F --> G
    G --> H
    H -->|"Yes"| I
    H -->|"No"| X["400 Invalid Token Type"]
    I --> J
    J -->|"Check"| L
    L --> M
    M -->|"Exists"| K
    M -->|"Not found/Revoked"| Y["401 Token Revoked"]
    K --> N
    N --> O
    O --> P
    P --> Q
    Q --> R
    R -->|"Yes (optional)"| S
    S --> T
    T --> U
    U --> V
    R -->|"No"| W

other_important_workflows.name: Multi-Channel Message Delivery Flow
other_important_workflows.description: A communication workflow supporting notification delivery through multiple channels (in-app push, SMS, email) with template support and delivery tracking. The workflow resolves recipient contact information from the account management domain, applies rate limiting for SMS to prevent abuse, coordinates with third-party providers (SMS gateway, email service), and persists message records for audit and user inbox functionality. This enables comprehensive patient-doctor communication for appointment reminders, health alerts, and care notifications.
other_important_workflows.flowchart_mermaid:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Trigger["Message Trigger"]
        A["System event triggers notification\n(appointment reminder, health alert, etc.)"]
        B["API request\nPOST /message/send"]
    end
    
    subgraph Consumer["Message Consumer"]
        C["MessageController"]
        D["Validate request parameters"]
        E{"Channel selection"}
    end
    
    subgraph Provider["Message Provider"]
        F["MessageServiceImpl.pushMessage()"]
        G["Create Message entity"]
        H{"Template specified?"}
        I["MessageTemplateService\nResolve & render template"]
        J["Persist to messages table"]
    end
    
    subgraph AccountLookup["Account Management"]
        K["UserIdentityAPI"]
        L["Retrieve recipient phone/email"]
    end
    
    subgraph PushChannel["In-App Push"]
        M["Store in user inbox"]
        N["Update unread count"]
    end
    
    subgraph SMSChannel["SMS Delivery"]
        O["SMSServiceImpl"]
        P["SMSRateLimiter\nCheck rate limit"]
        Q{"Within limit?"}
        R["Format SMS content"]
    end
    
    subgraph EmailChannel["Email Delivery"]
        S["EmailServiceImpl"]
        T["Format email content"]
        U{"Has attachments?"}
    end
    
    subgraph ThirdParty["Third-Party Services"]
        V["SMS Provider\n(external gateway)"]
        W["Email Provider\n(SMTP/API)"]
    end
    
    subgraph Tracking["Delivery Tracking"]
        X["Update delivery status"]
        Y["Record delivery timestamp"]
        Z["Return delivery result"]
    end
    
    A --> F
    B --> C
    C --> D
    D --> F
    F --> G
    G --> H
    H -->|"Yes"| I
    I --> J
    H -->|"No"| J
    J --> K
    K --> L
    L --> E
    
    E -->|"PUSH"| M
    M --> N
    N --> X
    
    E -->|"SMS"| O
    O --> P
    P --> Q
    Q -->|"Yes"| R
    Q -->|"No"| Z1["429 Rate Limited"]
    R --> V
    V --> X
    
    E -->|"EMAIL"| S
    S --> T
    T --> U
    U -->|"Yes"| W
    U -->|"No"| W
    W --> X
    
    X --> Y
    Y --> Z

other_important_workflows.name: User Logout and Token Revocation Flow
other_important_workflows.description: A security workflow ensuring secure session termination by invalidating both access and refresh tokens. When a user logs out, the access token is added to a Redis blacklist with TTL matching the token's expiration time, and the associated refresh token is removed from Redis storage. This prevents further use of the session tokens and supports scenarios like forced logout, password change, and security incident response. The workflow ensures that even valid tokens cannot be used after revocation.
other_important_workflows.flowchart_mermaid:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Client["Client Application"]
        A["User initiates logout"]
        B["Submit access token\nPOST /auth/logout"]
    end
    
    subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"]
        C["Route to auth-consumer"]
    end
    
    subgraph AuthConsumer["Auth Consumer"]
        D["SSOController\nReceives logout request"]
        E["SSOServiceImpl\nProcess logout"]
    end
    
    subgraph AuthProvider["Auth Provider"]
        F["TokenValidationServiceImpl\nrevokeToken()"]
        G["Parse access token"]
        H["Extract tokenId"]
        I["Extract expiration time"]
        J["Extract userId from claims"]
    end
    
    subgraph AccessTokenRevocation["Access Token Blacklist"]
        K["JwtUtils.addToBlacklist()"]
        L["Calculate remaining TTL\n(expiration - now)"]
        M["Add to Redis blacklist\nblacklist:{tokenId}"]
        N["Set TTL = remaining validity"]
    end
    
    subgraph RefreshTokenRevocation["Refresh Token Cleanup"]
        O["JwtUtils.revokeRefreshToken()"]
        P["Delete from Redis\nrefresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}"]
        Q{"Revoke all tokens?\n(forced logout)"}
        R["Delete all user refresh tokens\nrefresh_token:{userId}:*"]
    end
    
    subgraph Verification["Future Request Handling"]
        S["Subsequent API request\nwith revoked token"]
        T["TraceAndJwtAuthFilter"]
        U["TokenValidationServiceImpl\nvalidateToken()"]
        V{"Check blacklist"}
        W["Token found in blacklist"]
    end
    
    subgraph Response["Response"]
        X["Return logout success"]
        Y["Return 401 Unauthorized"]
    end
    
    A --> B
    B --> C
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E --> F
    F --> G
    G --> H
    H --> I
    I --> J
    J --> K
    K --> L
    L --> M
    M --> N
    N --> O
    O --> P
    P --> Q
    Q -->|"Yes"| R
    R --> X
    Q -->|"No"| X
    
    S --> T
    T --> U
    U --> V
    V --> W
    W --> Y
